name: Pre-Emergent + Post-Emergent
talk: Centipede and St. Augustine are dormant. Pre-emergent barrier plus broadleaf weed control. Note: lower Prodiamine rate than Bermuda — these grasses are more sensitive to pre-emergents.
yard: Dormant. Ideal time for weed prevention and soil conditioning.

name: Pre-Emergent + Post-Emergent
talk: Coming out of dormancy, starting to green up. Second pre-emergent chemistry plus continued broadleaf control.
yard: Greening up. Fewer weeds breaking through.

name: Fertilizer + Spot Treat
talk: Full green-up and active growth. Feeding the lawn with slow-release nitrogen plus iron for color and density. Spot-treating any weeds that got through.
yard: Actively growing, full green-up. Deeper color, thicker turf.

name: Soil Health + Post-Emergent
talk: Active growing season. Earth Max for soil biology plus Celsius for summer weed pressure. These grasses are thriving right now.
yard: Actively growing. Continued density and health improvement.

name: Fall Pre-Emergent
chems: Spot treat: C4 mix if needed
talk: Growth slowing down. Pre-emergent barrier for winter/spring weeds plus potassium for stress tolerance going into dormancy.
yard: Slowing growth. Winter/spring weed prevention begins.

name: Post-Emergent + Stress Relief
talk: Fully dormant. Cleaning up cool-season weeds and applying high-potassium stress relief for winter hardiness. Sets up a clean start in spring.
yard: Fully dormant. Winter weed prevention. Stress protection.
